Operation 'getClickData' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Get click data' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what parameters are available for filtering and what the response structure contains. Added specificity about filtering capabilities, response format (array of click entries), and clarified that this provides granular click-level data distinct from aggregated counters. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus getCounters.

data.current_description: Export raw click data with full parameters, timestamps and geolocation information. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour.
data.summary_suggestion: Export raw click data with geolocation details
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed click-level analytics data including timestamps, geolocation information, and request parameters for specified links. Supports filtering by date range, link ID, and other parameters. Returns an array of click entries with complete click event details. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Get click data' is too generic. Description lacks clarity about what parameters are available for filtering and what the response structure contains. Added specificity about filtering capabilities, response format (array of click entries), and clarified that this provides granular click-level data distinct from aggregated counters. This helps AI agents understand when to use this operation versus getCounters.

message: Operation 'getCounters' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary lacks specificity about aggregation. Description is too minimal and doesn't explain available filters or response structure. Added clarity about the aggregated nature of the data, filtering capabilities, response format, rate limiting context, and guidance on when to use this versus getClickData. This helps AI agents distinguish between aggregated metrics and detailed click data operations.

data.current_description: Get aggregated click counts. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves aggregated click count metrics for specified links, providing summary statistics rather than individual click details. Supports filtering by date range, link ID, and other parameters. Returns counter entries with total click counts and related metrics. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour. Use this operation for high-level traffic summaries; use getClickData for detailed click-level analytics.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks specificity about aggregation. Description is too minimal and doesn't explain available filters or response structure. Added clarity about the aggregated nature of the data, filtering capabilities, response format, rate limiting context, and guidance on when to use this versus getClickData. This helps AI agents distinguish between aggregated metrics and detailed click data operations.

message: Operation 'exportData' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, unclear_purpose. Original summary and description are vague about the export format and purpose. 'Export traffic report data' doesn't clarify what distinguishes this from other analytics operations or what format is returned. Added context about report formatting, filtering capabilities, response structure, and explicit differentiation from other analytics endpoints. This helps AI agents understand the specific use case for this operation and when it's appropriate versus alternatives.

data.current_description: Export traffic report data. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour.
data.description_suggestion: Generates and exports a formatted traffic analytics report for specified links. Supports filtering by date range, link ID, and other parameters. Returns traffic report data in a structured format suitable for analysis and reporting. Rate limited to 50 requests/hour. Differs from getCounters (aggregated metrics) and getClickData (raw click details) by providing formatted report output.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are vague about the export format and purpose.
